title: Visual refresh and layout/UX improvements: modern, writer-friendly UI
description:
Problem

The app feels outdated and the layout is confusing. We want a sleeker, more modern, smooth experience that writers prefer, with clearer structure and more intuitive flows.

Goals

* Visual refresh: Move from “outdated” to sleek, modern, and smooth. Keep the existing grayscale design system (src/styles/tokens.css); refine typography, spacing, shadows, and micro-interactions so the app feels contemporary and calm.
* Writer-focused UX: Optimize for long-form writing: comfortable reading and editing (font, line length, contrast), minimal distraction, clear hierarchy. Consider a “focus” or distraction-free mode if it fits the current architecture.
* Clearer layout and IA: Make navigation and structure more intuitive:
* Story editor (/story/\[id]): Three modes (Editor, Corkboard, Submissions) and a sidebar with Notes / Revision / Collab tabs. Improve discoverability of modes and panels, tab labels, and how users move between writing, outlining, and collaboration.
* Home (/): Quick Resume + Recent Stories. Clarify purpose of each block and primary actions.
* Stories list (/stories): Toolbar (New Story, search, archive toggle) and grid. Make actions and filters obvious and easy to use.
* App shell: Nav + header + content. Ensure global nav and page titles make “where I am” and “what I can do” obvious.

Tech context

* Next.js app; UI in src/components/ and src/app/; CSS modules + design tokens in src/styles/tokens.css.
* Use only the grayscale tokens (e.g. black-700, carbon-600, elephant-500, cement-400, fashion-300, fog-200, polar-100). Do not use deprecated gray-\* tokens.
* Key surfaces: AppShell, Navigation, Header, ContentContainer; story page layout and ViewModeSwitch; Corkboard, RevisionPassPanel, CollabPanel; StoryCard, SceneCard, forms and buttons.

Out of scope (for this ticket)

* New features or backend changes.
* Changing the grayscale-only constraint or adding new color systems (unless explicitly agreed).

Deliverables

1. Visual refresh: Updated typography, spacing, borders, shadows, and light motion/transitions where appropriate across the app, using existing tokens.
2. Layout/IA improvements: Clearer hierarchy, grouping, and labels on Home, Stories, and Story editor (including mode switcher and sidebar). No major new pages—improve current layout and navigation.
3. Writer-friendly polish: Editor and reading areas tuned for comfort (e.g. type scale, line height, max width); reduced visual noise; clear focus on the main writing surface.

Acceptance criteria

* App looks noticeably more modern and cohesive while staying within the grayscale palette.
* Navigation and story editor modes/panels are easier to understand and use.
* Writing and reading surfaces feel comfortable and intentionally designed for writers.
